questions.question: What is coordinate geometry?
questions.correct_answer: It is the study of geometry using coordinates on a plane.
questions.explanation: Coordinate geometry uses numbers to represent the position of points on a plane.

questions.question: What are the two axes in the Cartesian plane?
questions.explanation: The Cartesian plane is formed by two perpendicular number lines.

questions.question: What is the point of intersection of X-axis and Y-axis called?
How are coordinates of a point written?
questions.explanation: The first number represents x-coordinate and the second represents y-coordinate.

questions.question: How many quadrants are there in a Cartesian plane?
questions.explanation: The X-axis and Y-axis divide the plane into four regions called quadrants.

questions.question: In which quadrant does the point (3, 4) lie?
questions.explanation: Both x and y coordinates are positive.

questions.question: What is the distance of the point (2, −5) from the X-axis?
questions.explanation: Distance from X-axis is the absolute value of the y-coordinate.

questions.question: What is the distance of the point (−7, 3) from the Y-axis?
questions.explanation: Distance from Y-axis is the absolute value of the x-coordinate.

questions.question: Does a point on the X-axis belong to any quadrant?
questions.explanation: Points on axes do not lie in any quadrant.

questions.question: What are the coordinates of a point lying on the Y-axis?
questions.explanation: Any point on the Y-axis has x-coordinate equal to zero.

notes.title: Cartesian Plane
notes.content: The Cartesian plane is formed by two perpendicular number lines called the X-axis and Y-axis.

notes.title: Ordered Pair
notes.content: Coordinates of a point are written as an ordered pair (x, y), where x is the x-coordinate and y is the y-coordinate.

notes.title: Quadrants
notes.content: The plane is divided into four quadrants based on the signs of x and y coordinates.

facts.fact: The origin divides the plane into four equal quadrants.
Distance of a point from an axis is always a positive value.
